name: Saint-Clar and Mauroux – loop in the little French Tuscany
name_original: Saint-Clar et Mauroux – boucle dans la petite Toscane française
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Saint-Clar, located in the Gers region of France, traverse a landscape often described as "little French Tuscany." The area features rolling hills, scenic countryside, and meandering creeks, providing a varied backdrop for cycling. Predominantly paved surfaces characterize the cycling routes, with some segments offering a mix of road and gravel. This region is known for its picturesque villages and historical monuments, which cyclists can encounter along the way. Best touring cycling routes around Saint-Clar The most popular touring cycling route is Saint-Clar and Mauroux – loop in the little French Tuscany, a 39.3 miles (63.2 km) trail that takes 4 hours 8 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the rolling countryside between Saint-Clar and Mauroux. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Plieux and Flamarens Loop, a difficult 23.8 miles (38.3 km) path. This route offers views of the rural landscape and passes through the villages of Plieux and Flamarens. Local touring cyclists also love the Saint-Clar – Lectoure loop from Avezan, a 24.5 miles (39.5 km) trail leading through the Gers region's countryside, often completed in about 2 hours 34 minutes. Touring cycling around Saint-Clar is defined by rolling hills, scenic countryside, and picturesque villages. The network offers options for different ability levels, though many routes are rated as difficult. The routes in Saint-Clar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 190 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Saint-Clar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Saint-Clar?
A: There are over 50 touring cycling routes around Saint-Clar, offering a wide range of options for different preferences and fitness levels. The komoot community has explored these routes over 190 times.

Q: What makes touring cycling in Saint-Clar unique?
A: Touring cycling in Saint-Clar is often described as riding through a 'little French Tuscany.' You'll experience picturesque landscapes with rolling hills, scenic countryside, and meandering creeks. The routes frequently pass through charming villages and offer glimpses of historical monuments, providing a rich cultural and natural experience.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Saint-Clar?
A: The touring cycling routes in Saint-Clar are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.1 stars from more than 20 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the beauty of the Gers region's countryside, and the opportunity to explore quaint villages.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Saint-Clar?
A: Yes, Saint-Clar offers a few touring cycling routes suitable for beginners. While many routes are rated as difficult due to the rolling hills, there are 4 easy routes available that provide a gentler introduction to the region's beautiful landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes near Saint-Clar?
A: The terrain around Saint-Clar is characterized by rolling hills and scenic countryside. Routes are predominantly on paved surfaces, making them ideal for touring bikes. However, some segments may include unpaved sections, adding variety to your ride. Expect both gentle ascents and descents as you traverse the 'little French Tuscany'.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Absolutely. For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Saint-Clar offers 28 difficult touring cycling routes. These routes often feature significant elevation gains, with some reaching up to 1500 feet, and cover longer distances, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021113383/saint-clar-and-mauroux-loop-in-the-little-french-tuscany" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint-Clar and Mauroux – loop in the little French Tuscany&lt;/a&gt;, which is over 63 km long.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes around Saint-Clar?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Saint-Clar are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021112297/plieux-and-flamarens-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plieux and Flamarens Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38664355" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lectoure – Saint-Clar loop from Saint-Clar&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What interesting places can I see along the touring cycling routes near Saint-Clar?
A: As you cycle, you'll encounter picturesque villages and historical monuments. Notable attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3901046" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint-Orens and Saint-Louis Church&lt;/a&gt;, the charming &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3775277" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plieux Village&lt;/a&gt;, and the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3808248" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cathedral of St. Gervasius and St. Protasius in Lectoure&lt;/a&gt;. You might also pass by natural features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3201029" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake of the Three Valleys&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: When is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Saint-Clar?
A: The best time to go touring cycling in Saint-Clar is typically during spring, summer, and early autumn. These seasons offer pleasant weather to enjoy the region's picturesque landscapes, including potential sunflower fields or vineyards, making for a beautiful backdrop to your ride.

Q: Is parking available near the touring cycling routes in Saint-Clar?
A: Yes, parking is generally available in and around Saint-Clar, particularly in the village centers or designated parking areas, which serve as convenient starting points for many of the touring cycling routes.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling options in Saint-Clar?
A: While many routes feature rolling hills, there are easier options that can be suitable for families. Look for the 4 easy-rated routes, which offer shorter distances and less elevation gain, allowing families to enjoy the scenic countryside at a comfortable pace.

Q: Can I find places to eat or stay near the cycling routes?
A: Yes, the Gers region is known for its charming villages. Many touring cycling routes pass through or near villages like Saint-Clar, Mauroux, Plieux, and Lectoure, where you can find local cafes, pubs, and various accommodation options to refresh and rest during or after your ride.
tips:
The very pretty castle of Gramont belonged to Simon IV de Montfort, leader of the royal crusade against the Albigensians, who donated it to his crusade lieutenant, Odon de Montaut. The castle became a fortress in the 14th century with the construction of the Gothic entrance, then between 1535 and 1545 a Renaissance wing was built, placed at right angles to the old castle.
It was in the sixties that it was restored to regain its appearance.
Very pretty and old village which deserves the detour.

Large bastide with two arcaded squares and two historic centres, located on the old ridge road which links Gascony to the Pyrenees. Placed on a plateau overlooking the Arratz valley, the vialleg invites you to stroll. A few kilometers from the city center, you can also enjoy the Saint-Clar swimming lake!
